messages.content:
The participants will be asked to kindly complete the questionnaire. The questionnaire has five sections: Demographics information (e.g. the current working place , training  and education ). Section two asks about their general knowledge in hypermobility(e.g. the prevalence , whether it is inherited or not, associated features of joint hypermobility)
Section three asks about assessment of Joint Hypermobility and the related conditions (e.g. their confident level, assessment tools they use). Section four is about the management (e.g.the confident level, how mush they see each of the listed modalities effective). Finally, section five asks if they are keen to learn more about the condition and how to like to learn) in this section participant can share their email addresses if they want to be a part of the second part of the study. 
are there any typos ?
No, there are no typos.

what about this Actually, that is really hard to justify. I have discussed that with my supervisor, and we think that 50 participations will be a feasible number to analyze the data. However, to answer this question appropriately, I just changed the answer to “ A large sample is needed to be able to generalized the results, however, the maximum sample size needed can not be calculated”.

There are a few minor grammatical errors in the sentence. Here's a corrected version: 

"Actually, that is really hard to justify. I have discussed it with my supervisor, and we think that 50 participants will be a feasible number to analyze the data. However, to answer the question appropriately, I have revised my response to state that while a large sample size is needed to generalize the results, the maximum sample size required cannot be calculated."

Recruitment Plan:

1. Online advertising: We plan to recruit participants through online advertising on social media platforms, Twitter, Telegram and Whatsup . We will target specific demographics to reach potential participants who are likely to be interested in the study. That could be done by using my account on these platforms, and asking colleges to help with that. 



2. Online groups: We will try to identify and reach out to online groups that have a relevant audience and we will try to seek permission from admins or owners to post our study details. However, most of the online groups of Saudi physical therapy are not authorized. 

3. Email marketing trough Saudi Physical therapy Association (SPTA): they will help us sending out emails to their existing database of potential participants- Permission obtained 

4. Snowball sampling: We will kindly ask participants to help us with distributing the survey to their interested colleges or peers- will be informed in PIS .  


Sample Social Media Post:

Are you a physical therapist based in Saudi Arabia? We need your participation in our research study on joint hypermobility and its management. If you're interested in taking part, please complete our questionnaire. Help us spread the word by sharing this post with your colleagues and peers. Thank you for your support! #SaudiPhysicalTherapy #JointHypermobility #ResearchStudy #PhysicalTherapists

Yes, it might be a second part of the study which aims to address any gap of knowledge about joint hypermobility if there is any gap depending on the results of the study and measure the effectiveness. Separate ethical approval will be sought for the second part. Are there any typos?
No, there are no typos in this sentence.

Participation is open to any physiotherapists who treat children as a primary or partial part of their workload. Participants must be from Saudi Arabia. 

Do I have to take part?

No participation is entirely voluntary, and it is up to you to decide whether to take part or not. If you decide to take part, please agree and consent below to participate. If you decide not to take part, you do not have to explain your reasons and it will not affect your legal rights. 

What can be expected from participating?
The questionnaire will take less than 15 minutes to complete

What are the possible benefits of taking part?

There will be no direct advantages or benefits to you from taking part, but your contribution will help us understand the knowledge of physiotherapies about joint hypermobility , which will improve the current practice. 

Should I provide any personal information ?

No personally identifiable information is required unless you voluntarily offer personal or contact information in any of the comment fields. However, to maintain anonymous data collection, please make sure not to include any identifying information in the free text boxes. 
Please note we have dedicated a comment field for email address only if you wish to participate in future related research contributions you can provide your email. Please note your email address will be  used to contact you any time for future related research. However, it is not a mandatory section of this survey. Your email addresses will help us to reach out you and conduct further research in how to address/or improve your knowledge about this area and measure the effect size. 

Will my taking part in this project be kept confidential?

Yes. All ethical and legal guideline will be followed, and all information obtained will be kept strictly confidential and anonymous. To ensure confidentiality and anonymity, your email address will not be linked to the survey data and will not be included in the data analysis. 
are there any typos? and can you Paragraph the question and make the kind to participant to read.

Here's a re-phrased version of the text with paragraph breaks:

---

Thank you for your interest in this research project. 

We are inviting physiotherapists who treat children as a primary or partial part of their workload in Saudi Arabia to participate in our study. 

Participation is entirely voluntary, and you can choose whether or not to take part. If you decide to participate, please agree and consent below. If you choose not to take part, you don't need to provide an explanation, and it won't affect your rights. 

By participating in this study, you will be helping us to understand the knowledge of physiotherapists about joint hypermobility, which will help to improve current practice. 

The questionnaire will take less than 15 minutes to complete, and no personally identifiable information is required. Please ensure that you don't include any identifying information in the free text boxes to maintain anonymous data collection. 

If you wish to participate in future related research, you can provide your email address in the dedicated comment field. However, providing your email address is not mandatory. Your email address will only be used to contact you for future related research, and it will not be linked to the survey data or included in the analysis.

All the ethical and legal guidelines will be followed, and all the information obtained will be kept strictly confidential and anonymous. Confidentiality and anonymity will be ensured by keeping the survey data separate from any email addresses provided. 

Thank you for your consideration, and please let us know if you have any questions.
can you make thses question kind to the reader?

Certainly! Here's a more reader-friendly version:

---

Thank you for your interest in our research project!

Do you treat children as a primary or partial part of your workload, and are you based in Saudi Arabia? If so, we invite you to participate in our study.

Taking part is entirely up to you. If you decide to participate, it will involve completing a questionnaire that takes less than 15 minutes. You won't need to provide any personally identifiable information, and you're welcome to skip any questions that you're not comfortable answering.

Your participation will help us to understand the current level of knowledge among physiotherapists regarding joint hypermobility, and ultimately help to improve patient care. 

If you're interested in taking part, please read through the consent form and indicate your agreement. If you don't want to participate, that's absolutely fine! You don't need to provide an explanation, and it won't affect your rights.

Your confidentiality and anonymity will be completely respected throughout the study. Any data you provide will be handled according to strict ethical and legal guidelines to ensure your privacy.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to ask. Thank you again for your interest!
Who can participate?
We invite physiotherapists who treat children as a primary or partial part of their workload in Saudi Arabia to participate in our study.
Who can participate? re-write the question in a kind way
Are you a physiotherapist who treats children in Saudi Arabia? If so, we would be delighted to have you participate in our study!

Participant information sheet questions like Who can participate?Do I have to take part?What can be expected from participating?What are the possible benefits of taking part?Should I provide any personal information ?

Certainly, here's a reader-friendly version of each question:

1. Who can participate?
We would be delighted to have you participate if you are a physiotherapist based in Saudi Arabia who treats children as a primary or partial part of your workload.

2. Do I have to take part?
No, participation is entirely voluntary. It's up to you to decide whether or not you want to participate. If you do decide to take part, please read the consent form carefully and indicate your agreement.

3. What can be expected from participating?
You will be asked to complete a questionnaire that takes under 15 minutes. By participating, you'll be helping us to better understand the level of knowledge among physiotherapists regarding joint hypermobility, which will ultimately help to improve patient care.

4. What are the possible benefits of taking part?
You won't receive any direct benefits from participating in the study. However, your contribution will help us to better understand the knowledge of physiotherapists regarding joint hypermobility, which can help us to improve our current practice.

5. Should I provide any personal information?
No personally identifiable information is required unless you voluntarily offer personal or contact information in any of the comment fields. If you do provide your email address, it will only be used to contact you for potential future research studies and will be kept confidential and separate from the survey data.
Please indicate the kind of questions participants should expect.

The questionnaire will be divided into five sections:

Section 1: Demographics information - You will be asked to provide information about your current place of work, education, and training.

Section 2: General knowledge of hypermobility - You will be asked about your general knowledge of joint hypermobility, including its prevalence, associated features, and whether it is inherited or not.

Section 3: Assessment of Joint Hypermobility and related conditions - You will be asked about your confident level in assessing joint hypermobility and related conditions, as well as the tools you use to do so.

Section 4: Management - You will be asked about your confident level in managing joint hypermobility, and how effective you perceive various modalities to be.

Section 5: Interest in learning more - You will be asked if you're interested in learning more about joint hypermobility and how you'd like to learn. You'll have the option to share your email address if you're interested in participating in future related research.
